name: Roulade of Turkey with Foie Gras and Sauterne Sauce ...
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh foie gras (can be substituted with the same amount of chopped chicken liver)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove skin from turkey breast using a sharp knife.
2. Slice the turkey breast into 4 slices.
3. Pound each turkey slice between sheets of plastic wrap into paillardes (thin sheets).
4. Season the turkey with Kosher salt and cracked white pepper.
5. Divide foie gras (or chopped chicken liver) into 4 parts and season with salt and pepper.
6. Place a piece of foie gras in the center of each turkey paillarde and roll up the turkey around the foie gras.
7. Carefully remove cabbage leaves, first removing the core. Boil each leaf for about 3 minutes or until wilted, then refresh under cold running water and dry thoroughly.
8. For each roulade, lay out 2-3 cabbage leaves to form a square. Place the turkey roulade in the center and wrap with cabbage, forming a tight bundle. Refrigerate.
9. To prepare Sauterne sauce: Reduce 1 cup of Sauterne wine and 1 cup of chicken broth over high heat until it coats the back of a spoon (about 12 minutes). Sprinkle in 1/3 cup of sun-dried cherries.
10. For crispy sweet potatoes: Peel the potato, poach in boiling water for 12-15 minutes or until tender. Drain and slice very thin, then cut into julienne strips. Heat vegetable oil in a large sauté pan and fry potato strips for 1 minute or until crisp. Pat dry with paper towels and season with salt and pepper.
11. To serve: Steam the roulades exactly 15 minutes. The turkey will be cooked through, and the foie gras will be pink in the center. Reheat Sauterne sauce and spoon over each plate. Arrange crispy sweet potatoes around the roulade on warmed plates.
